meaning: n.阿拉伯式图饰,蔓藤花纹
examples: I like carpets with arabesque patterns.
It creates as a distance between two points, rather than a straight line, an arabesque.
Its forged and hammered metal had been wrought into elegant, almost arabesque, curves.
Calligraphy and the arabesque a continuous leaflike design dominate Islamic art, yet there are many figurative works here as well.
That such a legal arabesque would work may be a mark of what a challenge cloud computing will be in Europe.
